name: DFS with Bounds
summary: Carry a valid (lower, upper) range down the tree; each node must lie strictly inside it, and its children inherit tightened bounds.
visualizationIntent: init: build and show the input tree from the level-order array; every node idle. validate: a cursor ring lands on the current node (node_id) which turns amber (its frame is on the call stack); the node must lie strictly inside the inherited (lower, upper) range carried down from its ancestors. If value <= lower or value >= upper the node flips red (error) and the answer is false. Otherwise the left child inherits (lower, value) and the right child inherits (value, upper) — the valid window tightens as we descend — and a fully-validated node turns blue. The red node is the exact one that broke its inherited bound, even when its immediate parent looked fine. done: the boolean answer shows whether the whole tree is a valid BST.

lineExplanations.3: Count how many array slots describe the tree.
lineExplanations.4: Start an empty list of real node objects for the drawing.
lineExplanations.5: Start a lookup from a node's id to its object.
lineExplanations.6: Start a per-node state tracker: unseen, on-stack, done, or invalid.
lineExplanations.7: Loop over every slot to initialize its state.
lineExplanations.9: Point the child-slot walker just past the root.
lineExplanations.10: Loop over every slot to build the node objects.
lineExplanations.11: Only non-null slots become real nodes.
lineExplanations.12: Create this node with its value and two empty child links.
lineExplanations.13: Is there a slot left to be the left child?
lineExplanations.14: Is that candidate slot a real (non-null) node?
lineExplanations.15: Link that slot as this node's left child.
lineExplanations.16: Advance the walker past the left-child slot.
lineExplanations.17: Is there a slot left to be the right child?
lineExplanations.18: Is that candidate slot a real (non-null) node?
lineExplanations.19: Link that slot as this node's right child.
lineExplanations.20: Advance the walker past the right-child slot.
lineExplanations.21: Add the finished node to the tree.
lineExplanations.22: Record the node under its id for O(1) lookup.
lineExplanations.23: The root is slot 0, or nothing when the tree is empty.
lineExplanations.24: The lowest allowed value — a stand-in for negative infinity.
lineExplanations.25: The highest allowed value — a stand-in for positive infinity.
lineExplanations.26: Validate the whole tree, starting with the widest possible range.
lineExplanations.27: Return whether every node stayed inside its bounds.
lineExplanations.30: Base case: an absent child is trivially a valid BST.
lineExplanations.31: Return True for a missing node.
lineExplanations.32: Mark this node active — its frame is on the call stack.
lineExplanations.33: Read this node's value to test against the bounds.
lineExplanations.34: Is the value at or below the allowed lower bound?
lineExplanations.35: Out of range — mark this node invalid (red).
lineExplanations.36: The tree is not a BST; return False.
lineExplanations.37: Is the value at or above the allowed upper bound?
lineExplanations.38: Out of range — mark this node invalid (red).
lineExplanations.39: The tree is not a BST; return False.
lineExplanations.40: Validate the left child, tightening the upper bound to this value.
lineExplanations.41: Validate the right child, tightening the lower bound to this value.
lineExplanations.42: This node is valid only if both subtrees are.
lineExplanations.43: Both subtrees checked — mark this node fully validated.
lineExplanations.44: Return this subtree's verdict to the parent.

syntaxExplanations.1: class Solution: the wrapper class LeetCode expects.
syntaxExplanations.2: def isValidBST(self, values): the entry method; values is the tree as a level-order array.
syntaxExplanations.3: len(values) counts the elements in the input array.
syntaxExplanations.5: by_id = {} creates an empty dictionary (a map from id to node).
syntaxExplanations.6: state_by_id = [] will hold one status number per node id.
syntaxExplanations.7: for i in range(n): loops i from 0 to n-1.
syntaxExplanations.8: append(0) adds a 0 (unseen) entry for this slot.
syntaxExplanations.9: child = 1 sets the next-child pointer to slot 1.
syntaxExplanations.10: for i in range(n): a second pass, this time to build nodes.
syntaxExplanations.11: is not None checks the slot holds a real value.
syntaxExplanations.12: A dict literal { ... } builds this node object.
syntaxExplanations.13: if child < n: is there still a slot available?
syntaxExplanations.14: Check the candidate child slot is not null.
syntaxExplanations.15: node["left"] = child stores the left child's id.
syntaxExplanations.16: child = child + 1 advances to the next slot.
syntaxExplanations.17: if child < n: is there another slot for the right child?
syntaxExplanations.18: Check the candidate right slot is not null.
syntaxExplanations.19: node["right"] = child stores the right child's id.
syntaxExplanations.21: nodes.append(node) adds the built node to the list.
syntaxExplanations.22: by_id[i] = node registers the node under its id.
syntaxExplanations.23: A ? B : C-style conditional: 0 if there is a root, else None.
syntaxExplanations.24: low is an integer sentinel below every possible node value.
syntaxExplanations.25: high is an integer sentinel above every possible node value.
syntaxExplanations.26: self.validate(...) starts the bounded DFS at the root.
syntaxExplanations.27: return answer hands back the True/False verdict.
syntaxExplanations.29: def validate(self, node_id, lower, upper, by_id, state_by_id): the recursive checker; lower/upper are the allowed range for node_id.
syntaxExplanations.30: if node_id is None: the base case — an absent child.
syntaxExplanations.31: return True: an empty subtree satisfies the BST rule.
syntaxExplanations.34: value <= lower tests the lower bound (<= because values must be strictly greater).
syntaxExplanations.36: return False: the bound was violated.
syntaxExplanations.37: value >= upper tests the upper bound (>= because values must be strictly less).
syntaxExplanations.39: return False: the bound was violated.
syntaxExplanations.40: self.validate(...left...) recurses with upper tightened to value.
syntaxExplanations.41: self.validate(...right...) recurses with lower tightened to value.
syntaxExplanations.42: left_valid and right_valid: both sides must hold.
syntaxExplanations.43: state_by_id[node_id] = 2 marks the node fully validated.
syntaxExplanations.44: return result ends this call with the subtree's verdict.
